fix: gate VS extension job and harden marketplace version check in release workflow (#571)

- Add job-level \if\ to \ uild-visualstudio\ so it only runs on tag pushes
  or when \s_only == true\; manual VS Code-only releases no longer trigger
  the expensive VS extension build
- Fix null-array panic in the VS Marketplace version check: safely dereference
  \xtensions[0]\ before accessing \.versions\, treating a missing extension
  as a first-publish instead of falling through to the catch block
- Change the catch block from warn-and-proceed to hard-fail so a failed
  Marketplace query can no longer silently allow a duplicate-version publish

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add practice mode with spaced repetition

- New tab navigation: Diaria / Práctica
- Practice mode: 15 questions from 7 unique words with repetition
- Difficulty toggles: easy/medium/hard (at least one required)
- Word selection favors previously seen words, adds 2-3 unseen
- Shuffled queue avoids adjacent duplicate words
- Linear progress bar showing X/15 completion
- Per-word summary with fraction scores (e.g. 2/2 ✅)
- Practice history: shows today's last score, allows re-practice
- Results saved to localStorage per day
- Date navigation hidden in practice mode
- Mobile-responsive on all viewports

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: clean up and reorganize project structure

- Remove README.BACKUP.md (obsolete)
- Remove pro-test/ folder (old premium test branch)
- Remove blog-site/ folder (separate Astro project, 185MB)
- Remove .DS_Store system files (macOS artifacts)

REORGANIZATION:
- Move PORTFOLIO.md, POSITIONING.md to docs/guides/
- Organize docs/ into clear categories:
  * docs/guides/ - User guides (HOW_IT_WORKS, PORTFOLIO, POSITIONING, DESKTOP_APP)
  * docs/architecture/ - System design (ARCHITECTURE, MAP_ENGINE, AI_INTELLIGENCE, DATA_SOURCES)
  * docs/internals/ - Development docs (API specs, backend audit, roadmaps, legacy docs)
  * docs/assets/ - Images and media

Root organization:
- Keep essential docs at root: README.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
- Remove duplicate PORTFOLIO.md from root (now in docs/guides/)
- Remove duplicate POSITIONING.md from root (now in docs/guides/)

Result: Cleaner, easier to navigate, removes 50+ unused files, reduces noise

Fix: use sequential image generation to avoid API rate limiting

Fan-out per-image activities caused 429 rate-limit failures (4/5 images lost).
Replace GenerateSingleImageActivity with GenerateAllImagesActivity that generates
all images sequentially with built-in rate-limit delays. SEO + images still run
in parallel; image uploads still fan-out.

commit.message:
security: remove unauthenticated global JSON HTTP routes

The raw HTTP endpoints for global JSON had no token requirement
(except /post/global which had token check). They exposed all data
to anyone with network access, bypassing the auth layer entirely.

Removed routes:
  - GET  /get/global         (no auth)
  - GET  /get/global/path    (no auth)
  - POST /post/global        (had token but redundant)
  - POST /post/global/patch  (no auth)
  - POST /post/global/persist (no auth)

All global JSON operations are now only accessible via POST /post/run
with the proper commands (get_global_json, set_global_json,
patch_global_json, persist_global_json) which enforce token auth
for sensitive operations.

Updated README to remove the Global JSON Storage section from API docs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct Coder token API endpoint and permissions

- setup.sh: use /api/v2/users/me/keys/tokens (v2.31 correct endpoint)
- setup.sh: pre-create token file with chmod 0644 before Docker runs,
  preventing Docker bind-mount from creating it as a directory;
  0644 allows the non-root coder user inside containers to read it
- Dockerfile: push-template.sh explicitly loads CODER_SESSION_TOKEN
  from /run/secrets/coder-token so it works in non-interactive shells
  (e.g. AI agents) not just interactive bash sessions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover update() false-branch when assembled dir exists but checksum stale

Add test `update_with_stale_checksum_in_lockfile_reinstalls` to cover the
previously-uncovered False branch at installer/pipeline.rs:907:

The False case fires when the package directory exists in `.aipm/links/`
(from a prior install) but the stored lockfile checksum has changed since.
The test:
  1. Installs pkg-a to create the assembled directory and a correct lockfile.
  2. Tampers with the lockfile checksum to simulate a content change.
  3. Runs `update` — finds the dir exists but needs_update returns true,
     so the condition is false and the package is re-fetched.

Before: installer/pipeline.rs:907 branch 3 had 0 hits (uncovered).
After:  branch 3 is now covered; overall branch coverage 92.89% → 92.90%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: false unread badges on PWA reopen

On reconnect, session_list compares lastSeq vs readSeq. Non-content
messages (active, idle, tool_start, etc.) bump lastSeq after the user's
last markRead, causing false unread badges even though no new visible
chat bubbles exist.

Fix: scan IndexedDB for unread-worthy message types (error, permission,
question, idle-with-agent-message) between readSeq and lastSeq. Only
show badge if actual content the user should see exists.

Extract UNREAD_CANDIDATE_TYPES constant from message-router for shared
use between the live handler and the reconnect IDB scan.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace U+0094 control character with escaped inch mark in schematic JSON files (#200)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elliotttmiller/drywall-toolbox/sessions/00535967-807d-4958-b834-abcfe6d14df7

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: elliotttmiller <61537169+elliotttmiller@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add cache buster to getMagazines() to prevent stale CDN reads

getMagazines() was fetching blob URL without cache-busting query param,
causing Vercel CDN to serve stale/empty data. getUsers() already had
this fix (?t=Date.now()). Applied same pattern to getMagazines().

commit.message:
fix: align release image naming with HA addon config.json (#58)

The release workflow was pushing images to
ghcr.io/askb/ha-garmin-fitness-coach-addon:VERSION-ARCH but config.json
specifies image: ghcr.io/askb/garmincoach-addon-{arch}. HA resolves
{arch} at install time, so it expects per-arch image repos tagged with
the version.

Changes:
- Push to garmincoach-addon-ARCH:VERSION (matching config.json image field)
- Remove multi-arch manifest job (HA handles arch selection itself)
- Update SBOM scan to reference correct image name
- Add v0.10.0 CHANGELOG entry

Signed-off-by: Anil Belur <askb23@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: vercel.json rewrite breaking [id].js dynamic routes

The /api/(.*) catch-all rewrite was intercepting requests to dynamic
routes like /api/magazines/[id] and /api/users/[id], causing 404 for
all POST/PATCH/DELETE operations. Replaced with negative lookahead
pattern that only rewrites non-API paths to /public/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: complete agent-shield — 7 shield layers + adapters + CLI + benchmarks

Built by 5 AI agents across 3 waves (31 min, 12/12 tasks, 0 failures) via a2a-crews.

7 Shield Layers (OWASP Agentic Top 10):
- Input Sanitizer: prompt injection + jailbreak regex detection
- PII Filter: email/SSN/API key/phone regex with block/redact modes
- Scope Enforcer: tool allowlist/denylist + path validation
- Rate Limiter: token bucket per-agent, per-tool with burst
- Approval Gate: terminal/webhook/auto-approve modes
- Output Validator: unsafe content + hallucinated tool calls + schema
- Audit Logger: pino structured JSON for all decisions

Shield Class: shield.check(action) programmatic API
Framework Adapters: A2A JSON-RPC, MCP tool call, HTTP middleware
CLI: init, validate, serve, test commands
Benchmarks: sub-millisecond enforcement target

355 tests, 0 failures, 736 assertions, 1.4s

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Auto-resolve promises in TypeScript codegen to prevent silent RPC drops (#15901)

When users write un-awaited fluent chains like:
  const db = builder.addPostgres('pg').addDatabase('db');
  builder.addContainer('c', 'nginx').withReference(db);
  await builder.build().run();

the generated TypeScript now accepts PromiseLike<T> for handle parameters
(via Awaitable<T>) and automatically resolves them before RPC calls.
build() flushes all pending un-awaited promises before proceeding.

Key design decisions in transport.ts:
- trackPromise: .then(resolve, reject) removes from set on settlement.
  Reject handler swallows errors to prevent Node.js unhandled-rejection
  crashes. Errors are NOT eagerly collected — only promises still pending
  at flush time are observed, so user-caught rejections are not re-thrown.
- flushPendingPromises: snapshots the pending set before awaiting to
  prevent deadlock. The build promise is tracked by the PromiseImpl
  constructor while flush is suspended — without the snapshot, a while-loop
  re-check would find and await it, causing a circular await.
- Promise.allSettled collects all errors as AggregateError.

Testing:
- 8 vitest unit tests for trackPromise/flushPendingPromises covering
  deadlock prevention, error aggregation, and user-caught rejection safety
- E2E test with aspire start/stop validating runtime behavior
- 73 snapshot tests for generated code shape

Fixes #15899

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
